artPrompt: An unmarked door ajar onto a cramped back room, a flickering bare bulb swaying over a wobbling card table where shadowed goblin figures lean in close to strike a deal, hands half-clasped in the gloom; amber light pools on worn felt while cigar smoke and ink-black corners dissolve the edges, the perpetual hush of deals being struck, rendered as soft pastel impressionism — hazy diffuse light, visible chalky texture, gentle gradients, dreamy muted color.
name: The Back Room Where It Happens
text:
At the beginning of your upkeep, convene a meeting in the back room. Each opponent must either give you a snack, a Treasure, or do you a Favor (you create a Favor token). They choose, then you draw a card.
No minutes are kept. No minutes have ever been kept. The room does not officially exist.
flavorText: The deals are struck here, behind the unmarked door, beneath the flickering bulb, over the table that wobbles.
